prompt:
Flight context: Piper PA-28, VFR, training flight from KGEU (Glendale Municipal, AZ) — a small GA airfield with limited services. Planned 0900-1100 local (1600-1800 UTC). Review these NOTAMs and identify ALL operational hazards.

NOTAM 1: A0341/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QFUAU/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3334N11215W005
A) KGEU B) 2605150000 C) 2605172359
E) AVGAS 100LL NOT AVBL. SUPPLIER DELIVERY DELAYED.

NOTAM 2: A0782/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QNWAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3334N11215W005
A) KGEU B) 2605151200 C) 2605161800
E) AWOS-3 U/S.

NOTAM 3: A1093/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3334N11215W005
A) KGEU B) 2605160700 C) 2605161300
E) RWY 01/19 CLSD FOR CRACK SEAL. RWY 07/25 AVBL.

NOTAM 4: A1204/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QOBCE/IV/NBO/A/000/005/3334N11215W002
A) KGEU B) 2605140600 C) 2605302200
E) OBST CRANE ERECTED 0.2NM SOUTH APRT ELEV 1155FT (110FT AGL) UNLGTD.
gold_decision:
Four operational hazards:
1. AVGAS 100LL unavailable through 17 May 2359Z: PA-28 requires 100LL for operation. Flight cannot depart or refuel at KGEU during this period without pre-arranged fuel. Pilot must verify fuel state allows round-trip without refueling at KGEU, or divert to alternate fuel source (e.g., KCHD Chandler Municipal).
2. AWOS-3 unserviceable 1200-1800Z (0500-1100 local): covers the entire planned flight window. Automated weather is unavailable. Pilot must obtain weather via telephone briefing (1800WX-BRIEF), nearby ASOS, or Wx cameras. VFR flight with no automated weather at an uncontrolled airport increases risk of missing rapidly developing convective or wind-shift conditions.
3. RWY 01/19 closed 0700-1300Z: overlaps with planned departure (1600Z). If flight departs exactly at 1600Z this NOTAM has expired; however if departure is earlier or on May 16, RWY 01/19 is unavailable. Only RWY 07/25 available. Pilot must check runway alignment with wind.
4. CRANE OBSTACLE 0.2NM south of airport, 1155ft MSL / 110ft AGL, UNLIGHTED: active through May 30. Unlighted crane in the departure/approach path for RWY 19 (southbound). VFR pattern departure to the south must maintain obstacle clearance above 1155ft MSL. UNLGTD means no warning lights — hazard at twilight or in reduced visibility.
required_safety_constraints: AVGAS unavailability is a go/no-go item for a piston aircraft — no fuel means no legal dispatch
AWOS outage does not relieve VFR weather minimums — pilot must obtain weather by alternate means (14 CFR §91.103)
Unlighted crane at 110ft AGL in departure path is an immediate obstacle hazard — must be in pilot briefing
RWY 01/19 closure forces use of crosswind runway — crosswind component must be within aircraft limits
acceptable_variants: Candidate may note that AVGAS unavailability and weather outage together make the flight inadvisable on this day
RWY closure hazard may be noted as conditional on flight date/time
evidence_requirements: NOTAMs provided in prompt
14 CFR §91.103 — preflight action (weather, NOTAMs, fuel)
FAA JO 7930.2S Q-codes: QFUAU=fuel unavailable, QNWAS=weather reporting unserviceable, QMRLC=runway closed, QOBCE=obstacle crane erected

provenance.generation_rule: Small GA airfield scenario per T14 brief. KGEU Glendale Municipal is a real KZAB-FIR airport. AVGAS shortage scenario reflects real supply-chain disruptions. AWOS outage uses QNWAS (weather reporting system). Crane UNLGTD flag is safety-critical for RWY 19 south departure. All Q-codes per ICAO Annex 15 / FAA JO 7930.2S.

prompt:
Flight context: Cessna 182, VFR, cross-country to KSEZ (Sedona Airport, AZ) — 4,827ft MSL elevation, single runway, no tower. Planned arrival 1400Z. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0992/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QFAHE/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3441N11147W005
A) KSEZ B) 2605150900 C) 2605161700
E) AIRPORT ATTENDED HRS 1400Z-2300Z ONLY. UNATTENDED OUTSIDE THESE HRS.

NOTAM 2: A1103/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3441N11147W005
A) KSEZ B) 2605140000 C) 2605172359
E) RNAV (GPS) RWY 03 LNAV MINIMA NA. BARO ALTIMETER INOP.

NOTAM 3: A1445/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QNWAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3441N11147W005
A) KSEZ B) 2605151300 C) 2605151700
E) UNICOM 122.9 U/S. NO TRAFFIC ADVISORY SVC AVBL.

NOTAM 4: FDC 6/7821
Q) KZAB/QPIAU/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3441N11147W005
A) KSEZ B) 2605100000 C) 2605242359
E) RNAV (GPS) RWY 03 PROCEDURE AMDT. MDA RWY 03 LNAV RAISED TO 6040FT MSL (1213FT HAT). ALL OTHER MINIMA UNCHANGED.
gold_decision:
Four operational hazards:
1. RNAV LNAV minima NA due baro altimeter inop (NOTAM 2): LNAV approach to RWY 03 is not authorized. This is compounded by NOTAM 4 (FDC) raising the LNAV MDA; but since LNAV is NA entirely, only LNAV+V or LPV minima (if baro-independent) may apply. Pilot must verify GPS approach category availability without baro altimeter — at an uncontrolled high-elevation airport this is a significant limitation.
2. FDC NOTAM raises LNAV MDA to 6040ft MSL (1213ft HAT): Even if LNAV becomes available, MDA is significantly higher than charted. Must use 6040ft, not the charted MDA. This is a safety-critical procedure amendment.
3. UNICOM 122.9 unserviceable 1300-1700Z (covers arrival at 1400Z): No traffic advisory service available. Pilot must broadcast position on CTAF blind and maintain enhanced visual scan. At Sedona, traffic volume can be high due to scenery flights. Increased collision risk.
4. Airport attended only 1400-2300Z: Arrival at exactly 1400Z may find attendant just arriving. No fuel, services, or assistance outside attended hours. Pilot must ensure fuel sufficient for departure without KGSEZ refueling, or arrive within attended window.
required_safety_constraints: FDC procedure amendment supersedes printed chart — MDA must be 6040ft MSL not the charted value (FAA JO 7930.2S FDC hierarchy)
LNAV NA due inop baro altimeter removes primary non-precision instrument approach — VFR must be confirmed or divert
UNICOM outage at uncontrolled airport during arrival requires blind CTAF broadcasts and heightened visual traffic scan
High-elevation airport (4827ft MSL) with raised MDA means terrain clearance margins are reduced
acceptable_variants: Candidate may note that LPV minima (WAAS) are unaffected by baro altimeter inop — this is acceptable if correct
Candidate may flag both FDC NOTAM + LNAV NA as a single compound approach hazard
evidence_requirements: NOTAMs provided in prompt
FAA JO 7930.2S §5-1-9 — FDC NOTAM supersedes charted procedure
AIM 5-4-18 — RNAV approach types and baro requirement
14 CFR §91.129 / AIM 4-1-9 — CTAF procedures at uncontrolled airports

provenance.generation_rule: Small high-elevation GA airfield scenario. KSEZ Sedona is real (4827ft MSL, single RWY 03/21, no tower). LNAV NA due baro altimeter is a real NOTAM type. FDC MDA amendment constructed per FAA JO 7930.2S FDC format. QILAS reused for approach system degradation; QPIAU for procedure amendment per ICAO Annex 15.

prompt:
Flight context: Beechcraft Bonanza, VFR, fuel stop at KPGA (Page Municipal Airport, AZ) — remote desert GA airfield. Planned arrival 2100Z, departure 2200Z. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A0451/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QFUAU/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3653N11132W005
A) KPGA B) 2605171800 C) 2605181200
E) JET-A AND AVGAS SELF-SVC ONLY. CREDIT CARD SYSTEM U/S. CASH ONLY.

NOTAM 2: A0812/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QNWAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3653N11132W005
A) KPGA B) 2605160000 C) 2605192359
E) ASOS U/S. NO AUTOMATED SURFACE OBSERVATION AVBL.

NOTAM 3: A1122/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QLCAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3653N11132W005
A) KPGA B) 2605170600 C) 2605172200
E) APCH LGTG RWY 15 PAPI U/S.

NOTAM 4: A1389/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAB/QNMAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3653N11132W005
A) KPGA B) 2605170000 C) 2605172359
E) NOTAM SVC AFIS NOT AVBL. OBTAIN NOTAM INFO FM ALBUQUERQUE FSS.
gold_decision:
Four operational hazards:
1. Fuel self-service only, credit card system unserviceable: Pilot must carry sufficient cash for fuel. Remote desert airport — no alternate nearby. If pilot has no cash, fuel cannot be obtained. This affects go/no-go decision for the fuel-stop plan. Pilot should verify cash availability and self-service pump operation before departure.
2. ASOS unserviceable through May 19: No automated weather at KPGA for the entire planned stop. Pilot must obtain weather from Albuquerque AFSS or nearest reporting station. Desert canyon environment (Lake Powell area) can develop rapid afternoon convective activity and high winds; lack of local ASOS is a significant planning gap.
3. PAPI RWY 15 unserviceable 0600-2200Z: Arrival at 2100Z and departure 2200Z both fall within this window. PAPI is unserviceable, meaning visual glidepath guidance is unavailable for RWY 15. Night or dusk arrivals must use VASI (if available) or visual judgment. At a remote unlighted desert airport, glidepath reference loss is a safety concern.
4. NOTAM service (AFIS) unavailable — must contact Albuquerque FSS: This means standard NOTAM retrieval at KPGA is degraded. Pilot must proactively contact ABQ FSS for any updated NOTAMs. This NOTAM itself was obtained through FSS, suggesting the brief was correct. However, pilots relying solely on airport information system would miss late NOTAMs.
required_safety_constraints: Cash-only fuel at remote desert airport is a mission-critical service limitation — no cash means no fuel, potential stranding
ASOS outage at remote airport requires alternate wx source — not optional per 14 CFR §91.103 preflight requirements
PAPI outage at night or dusk requires heightened visual approach technique — increased CFIT risk at elevated terrain airport
AFIS unavailability means pilot must use alternative NOTAM source — cannot assume current NOTAM picture without FSS call
acceptable_variants: Candidate may note all four hazards compound at this remote airport with no nearby alternates
Candidate may identify PAPI outage as moderate (not critical) if arrival is planned in full daylight
evidence_requirements: NOTAMs provided in prompt
14 CFR §91.103 — preflight action requirements
FAA JO 7930.2S Q-codes: QFUAU=fuel unavailable, QNWAS=weather service U/S, QLCAS=PAPI unserviceable, QNMAS=NOTAM service unavailable

provenance.generation_rule: Remote GA airfield scenario. KPGA Page Municipal is a real isolated desert airport in KZAB FIR. Fuel self-service with credit-card outage reflects real operational limitation. PAPI outage uses QLCAS (lighting approach, condition AC=unserviceable). AFIS NOTAM service outage is a realistic scenario for remote airports.

prompt:
Flight context: UPS B767F, Part 121 cargo, KMEM (Memphis International) to KONT (Ontario, CA). Departure 0300Z (2200 local CST). KMEM is a major cargo hub with active night freight operations. Review these NOTAMs.

NOTAM 1: A2341/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZME/QMRLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3508N09000W005
A) KMEM B) 2605170200 C) 2605170500
E) RWY 36L/18R CLSD FOR FOD SWEEP AND PAVEMENT MARKING 0200-0500Z. RWY 36C, 36R AVBL.

NOTAM 2: A2567/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZME/QNFAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3508N09000W005
A) KMEM B) 2605170200 C) 2605170400
E) D-ATIS INFO AVBL ON 135.9 ONLY. ACARS DATALINK D-ATIS U/S.

NOTAM 3: A1891/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZME/QIGAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3508N09000W005
A) KMEM B) 2605160600 C) 2605180600
E) ILS RWY 18C GS U/S. LOC ONLY.

NOTAM 4: A3012/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZME/QLTLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3508N09000W005
A) KMEM B) 2605170100 C) 2605170600
E) TWY ALPHA 5 AND ALPHA 6 LGTG U/S. USE FOLLOW-ME OR GROUND CTL GUIDANCE FOR CARGO RAMP ACCESS.

NOTAM 5: A2788/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZME/QMXLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3508N09000W005
A) KMEM B) 2605170000 C) 2605172359
E) CARGO RAMP EAST SECTOR CLSD 0000-0700Z LCL TIME CONVERSION: 0000-0700Z = 1800-0100 CDT PREV NIGHT.
gold_decision:
Five operational hazards for 0300Z departure from KMEM cargo hub:
1. RWY 36L/18R closed 0200-0500Z: Primary long runway unavailable during 0300Z departure window. UPS heavy cargo operations must use RWY 36C or 36R. Performance calculations must be revalidated for alternate runway length, obstacle clearance, and weight limits.
2. D-ATIS ACARS datalink unserviceable 0200-0400Z: ACARS ATIS pull is unavailable — standard cargo operations rely on ACARS for ATIS. Crew must tune 135.9 for D-ATIS. Failure to obtain correct ATIS prior to pushback is an ATC compliance and safety issue.
3. ILS RWY 18C glideslope unserviceable: Departure runway is not the immediate issue but return or divert to KMEM with 18C ILS would be LOC-only. Crew should note this for en-route fuel planning if KMEM is an alternate.
4. Taxiway Alpha 5/6 lighting unserviceable 0100-0600Z: Covers entire taxi period. Night cargo ramp access to Alpha 5/6 is unlighted — requires follow-me vehicle or ground control coordination. This is procedurally critical at a 24-hour cargo hub where ramp coordination is time-sensitive.
5. Cargo Ramp East Sector closed 0000-0700Z: LCL annotation: closes at 1800 CDT previous evening through 0100 CDT. At 0300Z (2200 CDT prior night), this NOTAM is ACTIVE. East Sector closure affects gate assignment and pushback routing for eastside cargo operations.
required_safety_constraints: RWY 36L/18R closure during departure window requires immediate runway reassignment and new performance calculations — safety critical for heavy cargo
ACARS ATIS outage means crew must use voice ATIS — failure to get ATIS before pushback violates ATC phraseology norms and could cause confusion on ground freq
LCL time annotation on Cargo Ramp closure must be correctly converted — 0000-0700Z covers 0000-0700 UTC, LCL annotation is informational only; primary time field (B/C) governs
Taxiway lighting outage at night requires follow-me — crew cannot proceed to Alpha 5/6 without coordination
acceptable_variants: Candidate may correctly identify that cargo ramp closure LCL annotation means 1800-0100 CDT which has already passed, and the UTC window (0000-0700Z) governs — if flight is at 0300Z, ramp is still closed
Candidate noting 5 NOTAMs with 5 distinct operational impacts is the expected answer
evidence_requirements: NOTAMs provided in prompt
FAA JO 7930.2S §2-1-5 — LCL time annotation interpretation
ICAO Annex 15 Q-codes: QMRLC=runway closed, QNFAS=ATIS datalink U/S, QIGAS=ILS glideslope, QLTLC=taxiway lighting, QMXLC=apron/ramp closed

Night operations 0300Z window chosen to stress multiple simultaneous NOTAM interactions. LCL time annotation on cargo ramp NOTAM tests T14 requirement for LCL conversion knowledge. ACARS ATIS outage is a realistic cargo ops scenario. All Q-codes per FAA JO 7930.2S.

A) KSDF B) 2605170400 C) 2605170700

NOTAM 2: A1456/26 NOTAMN
Q) KSDF/QLTLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3811N08545W005
A) KSDF B) 2605170300 C) 2605170600
E) TWY CHARLIE EDGE LGTG U/S BTWEEN TWY ECHO AND TWY FOXTROT.

NOTAM 3: A0987/26 NOTAMN
Q) KSDF/QILAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3811N08545W005
A) KSDF B) 2605160600 C) 2605181800
E) ILS RWY 35R LOC U/S.

NOTAM 4: A2210/26 NOTAMN
Q) KSDF/QNFAS/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3811N08545W005
A) KSDF B) 2605170415 C) 2605170445
E) ATIS FREQUENCY 124.2 U/S. USE 124.6.

NOTAM 5: A3041/26 NOTAMN
Q) KSDF/QSTLC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/3811N08545W005
A) KSDF B) 2605170400 C) 2605171200
E) APCH LGTG REIL RWY 17R U/S.
gold_decision:
Five operational hazards for 0430Z departure from KSDF:
1. RWY 17L/35R closed 0400-0700Z: KSDF primary heavy cargo runway is unavailable at the 0430Z departure. B777F must use RWY 17R or RWY 29. Performance calculations must be rerun for the available runway. Weight and balance may need adjustment.
2. ILS RWY 35R LOC unserviceable: Even after 0700Z when the runway reopens, ILS to RWY 35R is unavailable through 18 May 1800Z. No ILS return available to primary cargo runway if divert is needed.
3. Taxiway Charlie edge lighting unserviceable between Echo and Foxtrot (0300-0600Z): Covers taxi period. Night taxi on TWY Charlie between E and F is degraded. Ground controller must advise of lighting outage; crew must use enhanced vigilance or follow-me.
4. ATIS 124.2 unserviceable 0415-0445Z: Very narrow window (30 min) centered on the 0430Z departure. Primary ATIS frequency is unavailable exactly at departure time. Must use backup 124.6. Risk of crew missing ATIS if they attempt on wrong frequency and proceed without weather update.
5. REIL RWY 17R unserviceable 0400-1200Z: If departing on RWY 17R (alternate due to 17L/35R closure), the runway end identifier lights are out. Night departure on RWY 17R without REIL means runway identification is reduced. Enhanced runway confirmation procedures required.

Two ILS LOC outages (35R) mean cargo return or divert to KSDF would use non-precision approach

prompt:
Flight context: B747-400F, Part 121 cargo, PANC (Anchorage, AK) to KORD (Chicago), oceanic/polar crossing, departure 1200Z. ETOPS-207 authorized. Review these NOTAMs and identify hazards relevant to the oceanic crossing.

NOTAM 1: A0341/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAK/QGAAA/IV/NBO/E/000/999/6200N15000W200
A) KZAK B) 2605170800 C) 2605172000
E) GPS UNRELIABLE WITHIN 200NM OF 6200N15000W DUE TO SATELLITE MAINTENANCE. ESTIMATED DURATION 8 HRS. IRS NAVIGATION REQUIRED.

NOTAM 2: A0892/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAK/QXXXX/IV/NBO/E/000/999/6400N13500W150
A) KZAK B) 2605170000 C) 2605172359
E) OCEANIC TRACK SYSTEM NORTH PACIFIC COMPOSITE (NOPAC) OTS AMBER MODIFIED. TRACK Y SUSPENDED DUE WEATHER. TRACKS ALPHA-DELTA AVBL VIA ATC COORDINATION.

NOTAM 3: A1233/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAK/QRTCA/IV/NBO/W/000/600/7200N14000W300
A) KZAK B) 2605171000 C) 2605171800
E) R-2203 ACT SFC TO FL600. SPECIAL OPERATIONS. AVOID.

NOTAM 4: FDC 6/4421
Q) KZAK/QPIAU/IV/NBO/E/000/999/6000N15800W050
A) PANC/KZAK B) 2605100000 C) 2605312359
E) PANC DEPARTURE PROCEDURE AMDT. PANDA THREE DEPARTURE RWY 07L: INITIAL CLIMB ALTITUDE CHANGED FROM 3000FT TO 4000FT MSL. TERRAIN CLEARANCE.
gold_decision:
Four operational hazards for PANC-KORD polar/oceanic crossing:
1. GPS unreliable 200NM radius of 6200N15000W, 0800-2000Z: Departure at 1200Z means aircraft will enter this GPS-degraded zone during the Pacific crossing. ETOPS operations require accurate navigation — IRS must be primary navigation system in this area. ETOPS 207 ops specs require GPS for oceanic navigation currency; if IRS-only, crew must verify IRS alignment and ops spec compliance for IRS-only oceanic ops.
2. NOPAC OTS Track Y suspended: Oceanic Track Y is unavailable. Flight plan must be revised to use Tracks Alpha-Delta via ATC coordination. If flight plan includes Track Y, it must be amended before departure. ATC coordination required for alternate track assignment.
3. R-2203 active SFC-FL600, 0800-1800Z: Large restricted airspace (300NM radius) active during crossing window. Polar routing must avoid this block. At FL600 and below, aircraft cannot penetrate. Since this covers SFC-FL600, ALL flight levels are prohibited. Ops must coordinate a route avoiding R-2203 entirely.
4. FDC amends PANDA THREE departure: Initial climb altitude changed to 4000ft MSL for RWY 07L. This is a terrain clearance amendment — the old 3000ft figure must not be used. B747F crew must have this amendment loaded in FMS or briefed for initial departure from PANC RWY 07L.
required_safety_constraints: GPS degradation in ETOPS corridor requires IRS-only navigation capability verification against ops specs (14 CFR §121.647)
NOPAC track suspension requires flight plan amendment before departure — ATC will not accept Track Y
R-2203 SFC-FL600 means NO altitude avoids it — lateral routing change only
FDC departure procedure amendment is safety-critical terrain clearance change — must use 4000ft not 3000ft
acceptable_variants: Candidate may note that GPS NOTAM and R-2203 together significantly constrain the northern routing options
Candidate may flag IRS-only navigation as requiring dispatch release amendment under Part 121

FAA JO 7930.2S §5-1-9 — FDC departure procedure amendment

A) KZAK B) 2605170000 C) 2605172359

NOTAM 2: Q0441/26 NOTAMN
Q) NZZO/QRTCA/IV/NBO/W/000/999/1000S17000W200
A) NZZO B) 2605170800 C) 2605172000
E) SPECIAL USE AIRSPACE ACT. MARITIME EXERCISE. 200NM RADIUS 1000S17000W. COORDINATE WITH AUCKLAND OCEANIC CTR ON 129.9.

NOTAM 3: FDC 6/5531
Q) KZAK/QPIAU/IV/NBO/E/000/999/3400N11825W050
A) KLAX B) 2605150000 C) 2605302359
E) KLAX DEPARTURE PROCEDURE AMDT. LOOP THREE DEPARTURE: PUBLISHED ALTITUDE AT COMPTON VOR CHANGED FROM FL180 TO FL240 FOR ALL ACFT. ATC SEPARATION REQUIREMENT.

NOTAM 4: A4112/26 NOTAMN
Q) KZAK/QXXXX/IV/NBO/E/000/999/2800N15000W300
A) KZAK B) 2605171800 C) 2605180600
E) PACOTS TRACK KILO MODIFIED. WAYPOINTS CHANGED: DELETE TROUT, INSERT WOLOG. REVISED TRACK FILE ISSUED VIA ATC. COORDINATION REQUIRED.
gold_decision:
Four hazards for KLAX-YSSY ETOPS-330 oceanic crossing:
1. GPS unreliable FL250-FL660 within 150NM of 1500N16000W (solar radio burst): Active entire crossing day. ETOPS-330 long-range oceanic navigation requires GPS in this mid-Pacific area. Crew must use IRS/inertial navigation backup and verify position cross-checks. Ops specs require notification to dispatch; some operators require MEL deferral of GPS-dependent procedures.
2. Auckland Oceanic FIR (NZZO) special use airspace active 200NM radius near 1000S17000W, 0800-2000Z: Arriving in NZZO jurisdiction during descent. Must coordinate with Auckland Oceanic on 129.9. Penetration without coordination violates ICAO airspace procedures. Route may need lateral deviation.
3. FDC amends KLAX LOOP THREE departure: Compton VOR altitude changed to FL240 (from FL180). If LOOP THREE is the assigned departure procedure, crew must use FL240 restriction. This is an ATC separation amendment — using wrong altitude could create IFR conflict with other departures.
4. PACOTS Track Kilo modified: Waypoints changed (TROUT deleted, WOLOG inserted). If Track Kilo is filed, the flight plan must be updated to reflect the revised waypoints. ATC will use the revised track — aircraft navigating old Track Kilo waypoints will be off-route. Dispatch must coordinate revised track file.

Auckland Oceanic FIR (NZZO) coordination is an international airspace entry requirement — failure is an ICAO violation

PACOTS waypoint change on filed track must be updated before departure or aircraft will navigate off-route
